name: Parque Natural de Despeñaperros — round trip through the Collado de los Jardines and Mirador Estacion de Santa Elena
name_original: Parque Natural de Despeñaperros — ida y vuelta por el Collado de los Jardines y Mirador Estacion de Santa Elena
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santa Elena are primarily found in the Sierra Morena region of Spain, characterized by mountainous landscapes and natural parks. The terrain features significant elevation gains, with routes traversing through areas like the Parque Natural de Despeñaperros. Surfaces are mostly well-paved, making them suitable for road biking, though some segments on more challenging routes may include unpaved sections. This region offers a variety of routes for different fitness levels, from moderate climbs to more demanding long-distance rides. Best road cycling routes around Santa Elena The most popular road cycling route is Parque Natural de Despeñaperros — round trip through the Collado de los Jardines and Mirador Estacion de Santa Elena, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route offers mountainous landscapes and panoramic views within the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Aliseda – Collado de los Jardines loop from Río Despeñaperros, a difficult 50.3 miles (81.0 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gains, providing a challenging ride through the region's varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Vadollano Roman Bridge – Monument Navas De Tolosa loop from La Carolina, a 45.5 miles (73.2 km) trail leading through historical landmarks and natural scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 29 minutes. Road cycling around Santa Elena is defined by mountainous landscapes, natural parks, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santa Elena?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Santa Elena, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 3 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 11 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: The road cycling routes in Santa Elena, primarily located in the Sierra Morena region of Spain, are characterized by mountainous landscapes and significant elevation gains. Surfaces are mostly well-paved, making them suitable for road biking, though some segments on more challenging routes may include unpaved sections within areas like the Parque Natural de Despeñaperros.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santa Elena?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and natural park settings, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Santa Elena. Temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be quite hot, especially in the Sierra Morena region, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, though cycling is possible year-round.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Santa Elena offers routes suitable for beginners. While many routes feature significant elevation, there are 3 designated easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Elena Fountain – La Aliseda loop from Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option that is shorter and has less elevation gain compared to the more challenging rides.

Q: What makes the road cycling routes in Santa Elena challenging?
A: The challenging nature of road cycling in Santa Elena stems from its mountainous landscape, particularly within the Parque Natural de Despeñaperros. Routes often feature substantial elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Aliseda – Collado de los Jardines loop from Río Despeñaperros&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 81.0 km with 1,450m of elevation, providing a demanding ride for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, many routes pass by significant landmarks and offer stunning viewpoints. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón de Despeñaperros Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5418434" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4215341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Órganos&lt;/a&gt;, all offering panoramic views of the Sierra Morena. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185478" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vadollano Roman Bridge – Monument Navas De Tolosa loop from La Carolina&lt;/a&gt; also leads through historical sites.

Q: Can I see any natural features like waterfalls while road cycling?
A: While primarily focused on road cycling, some routes or nearby areas offer access to natural features. For instance, the region around Santa Elena features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5289848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Negrillo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Fraile Waterfall and Spring&lt;/a&gt;, which could be visited as detours or part of a multi-sport exploration.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Santa Elena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1274417571/parque-natural-de-despenaperros-round-trip-through-the-collado-de-los-jardines-and-mirador-estacion-de-santa-elena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Despeñaperros — round trip through the Collado de los Jardines and Mirador Estacion de Santa Elena&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Elena Fountain – Miranda del Rey loop from La Carolina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Santa Elena?
A: The road cycling routes in Santa Elena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santa Elena's varied terrain, often praising the mountainous landscapes and panoramic views within the natural parks.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in Santa Elena?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Santa Elena, particularly near popular starting points for routes within the Parque Natural de Despeñaperros and in nearby towns like La Carolina. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions for detailed parking information.

Q: How can I access the road cycling routes using public transport?
A: While direct public transport to every trailhead might be limited, Santa Elena is accessible by regional bus services. Major towns like La Carolina, which serve as starting points for several routes, often have better public transport connections. From these towns, you can cycle to the route starting points.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small towns and villages where you can find local cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Within the Parque Natural de Despeñaperros, options might be more spread out, so it's wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

Q: Are the road cycling routes in Santa Elena suitable for families?
A: While the region is characterized by mountainous terrain and significant elevation, there are easier routes that might be suitable for families with older, more experienced children. However, due to the general difficulty and traffic on some roads, families with young children might prefer less challenging, dedicated cycling paths if available, or shorter, less elevated sections of routes.
tips: The Mirador de la Estación de Santa Elena in the Sierra Morena is a spectacular spot to enjoy panoramic views of the region. This viewpoint offers stunning views of the mountainous landscapes and vegetation typical of the Sierra Morena. It is a popular destination for nature and hiking lovers, providing a peaceful and rejuvenating experience.

Incredible views over the geological mountain gorge that joins La Mancha and Andalusia within the Desfiladero de Despeñaperros Natural Park. Going down the Despeñaperros river is fun, but save energy for the climb.
It is a beautiful stretch, perfect for riding the bike and enjoying the views. There are six kilometers of ascent from the Despeñaperros river.

waypoints.name: View Toward Andalucía – Despeñaperros Natural Park
